resolved_problems.title: Fan-out subscription with single-consumer mpsc::Receiver
resolved_problems.description: Rust's std::sync::mpsc::Receiver is single-consumer; Controller::subscribe() needs to deliver Status updates to multiple independent callers simultaneously.
resolved_problems.resolution: MockController maintains a Vec<SyncSender<Arc<Status>>> in Inner. On publish, all senders are attempted; failed sends (dropped Receivers) are lazily removed via retain().

resolved_problems.title: Go errors.Is() chain vs Rust error handling
resolved_problems.description: Go's errors.Is(ErrBusy, ErrTimeout) returns true because ErrBusy wraps ErrTimeout. The Rust Error::source() chain could replicate this but is non-idiomatic.
resolved_problems.resolution: Implemented is_relay_*() helper methods on RcpError using matches!() macro. ErrBusy.is_relay_timeout() returns true by pattern matching both Timeout and Busy variants. More explicit and zero cost.

resolved_problems.title: Zero-copy loan with RAII release
resolved_problems.description: LoaningController requires zero-copy buffer return. Go uses GC; Rust needs explicit return.
resolved_problems.resolution: Loan struct holds Option<Box<dyn FnOnce(Vec<u8>) + Send>>. Calling return_loan() takes the release function; Drop does nothing (caller must explicitly return or use send_loaned). LoanPool uses Condvar-based blocking acquire.

resolved_problems.title: Priority queue dispatch thread lifetime
resolved_problems.description: PrioController's dispatch thread must outlive the controller but not leak.
resolved_problems.resolution: Thread holds Arc<(Mutex<Queues>, Condvar)>. When PrioController is dropped, Queues is closed and Condvar is notified; dispatch thread sees closed=true with empty queues and exits.
